articles.title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
articles.description:
After years of cheering runners in Wellesley, 18-year-old Emmie Laudano will run Boston to support the Tierney Learning Center.

The post Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Wellesley senior Emmie Laudano, 18, will run her first Boston Marathon.
Laudano aims to support the Tierney Learning Center during the race.
Her participation highlights community engagement and support for local education.

articles.title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
articles.description:
A Gaga surprise, a Beverly break-in, and a Hulu hunk all make this week’s news quiz.

The post Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Beverly experienced a break-in, raising concerns about local safety.
Lady Gaga made a surprise appearance in Boston, delighting fans on April 1.
Hulu's new series features Boston locations, boosting local tourism and visibility.

articles.title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
articles.description:
"We didn't respect the Stanley Cup champion. And that was it. That was the game."

The post Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Marco Sturm criticized Bruins for lack of respect towards Panthers in recent playoff loss.
Bruins lost to defending champions Panthers, highlighting a need for improved team focus.
Local fans express disappointment as Bruins' playoff hopes diminish after this critical defeat.

articles.title: No mortgage, no insurance: Why some Massachusetts homeowners are choosing to ‘go bare’
articles.description:
Especially along the coast, a growing number of homeowners are gambling on having no insurance.

The post No mortgage, no insurance: Why some Massachusetts homeowners are choosing to ‘go bare’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Homeowners along Massachusetts' coast are increasingly opting for no insurance coverage.
This trend is particularly noticeable in Cape Cod, affecting local property values.
Residents risk significant financial loss in case of natural disasters without insurance.
